clause.text: If a credit card is not activated within 30 days of issuance, the issuer must seek OTP-based consent; absent consent, the account must be closed without cost within 7 working days of seeking confirmation.

check.description: Days-since-issuance comparison gating an OTP-consent workflow; days-since-confirmation comparison gating forced closure.

candidate_locus.note: card-activation batch program in GnuCOBOL-native base; CardDemo has no activation workflow

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.

candidate_locus.note: COMPUTE statement in a penalty/adjustment batch program; GnuCOBOL-native base

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.

check.description: Over-limit gate on transaction posting: projected balance vs ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT, with consent flag required to permit breach.

candidate_locus.note: shipped overlimit check; consent-flag dimension absent in CardDemo (itself a candidate D2)

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

check.description: Working-day window computation for closure SLA; per-calendar-day penalty accrual on breach.
check.t6_note: value-change pair: pre-2024 working-day basis -> 2024 calendar-day basis (carried into 2025 para 19). Pre-amendment 2022 wording MUST be taken from the archived 2022 PDF at T2.5, not memory

candidate_locus.note: closure batch program computing penalty accrual; GnuCOBOL-native base

prior_2022.note: calendar-day basis introduced by the 2024-03-07 amendment to the 2022 MD

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2022 old-side text pending archive; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: If a card is unused for more than one year, initiate closure after intimating the cardholder; if no reply within 30 days, close the account (subject to dues) and update the CIC within 30 days.

check.description: Inactivity-duration comparison driving a closure workflow; account-status transition and CIC notification deadlines. NB: 2025 para 20 is unused-card closure (the 2022 MD para 20 was co-branding outsourcing) -- the renumbering that seeded the earlier clause-20/29 confusion.

candidate_locus.note: the naturally-missing ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS check (T0.2 D2 locus) is the code-side face of closed/blocked-account enforcement

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Minimum Amount Due must be stipulated so there is no negative amortization; unpaid charges, levies and taxes shall not be capitalized for charging or compounding of interest.

check.description: Interest-base construction: the balance on which interest is computed must exclude unpaid charges/levies/taxes; MAD formula must cover at least accrued interest+charges.

candidate_locus.note: interest computed on TRAN-CAT-BAL; a mutation folding fees into the base is the drift

prior_2022.note: took effect 2022-10-01 per the 2022 MD FAQ Q7

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: A card account may be reported past-due to CICs or levied penal charges only when past due for more than three days; days-past-due and late charges are computed from the payment due date; late charges apply only to the outstanding amount after the due date, not the Total Amount Due.

check.description: Strictly-greater-than-3-days comparison gating penal action (canonical > vs >= host); late-charge base variable selection (outstanding vs total-amount-due).
check.t6_note: pre-2024 version lacked the 3-day rule and TAD was undefined; pair with CC-03a-xxi

candidate_locus.note: late-fee assessment batch; GnuCOBOL-native base; CBACT04C group-file pattern reusable

prior_2022.note: 3-day rule + TAD basis introduced by the 2024-03-07 amendment

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Interest shall be levied only on the outstanding amount, adjusted for payments, refunds and reversed transactions.

check.description: Interest-base must net out credits before computation; ordering of adjustment vs interest computation in the batch flow.
check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Changes in charges take prospective effect only, with prior notice of at least one month.

check.description: Effective-date comparison between charge-change date and notice date (>= 1 month).
check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Bills must be sent without delay and the customer must have at least one fortnight for payment before interest begins to be charged.

check.description: Gap between statement date and interest-start date must be >= 15 days.

candidate_locus.note: statement/interest-cycle batch; CBSTM03A statement program is a structural template

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: If a cardholder protests a bill, the issuer must provide an explanation (with documentary evidence where applicable) within a maximum of 30 days from the date of complaint.

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Explicit consent is required to adjust credit (from refund/failed/reversed transactions already paid for) beyond a cut-off of one percent of the credit limit or Rs 5000, whichever is lower; consent sought within 7 days of the credit; on cardholder request, reversal to the bank account within 3 working days.

check.description: min() threshold computation mixing a percentage and an absolute amount; two SLA windows. Richest single-clause COMPUTE host in the corpus.

candidate_locus.note: refund-adjustment batch; cutoff constants belong in a copybook (natural MO-1x host)

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: Total Amount Due is the total amount (net of credit received during the billing cycle, if any) payable by the cardholder as per the statement generated at the end of a billing cycle.

check.description: Definitional anchor for charge-base selection; pre-2024 the term was undefined.

candidate_locus.note: pairs with CC-09b-v late-charge base logic

check.verification: 2025 para secondary-mapped (taxguru repro 2026-07-09); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD; 2025 primary PDF due T2.5

clause.text: If a complainant does not get a satisfactory response within a maximum period of 30 days from lodging the complaint, they may approach the RBI Ombudsman.

check.description: Grievance-aging SLA comparison; pre-2024 text said 'one month' (28-31 day ambiguity) vs exact 30 days.
check.t6_note: month-vs-30-days is a boundary-semantics pair; verify 2022 wording from archived PDF at T2.5

prior_2022.note: 2022 side said 'one month'; pending archive pull

clause.text: If a cardholder settles dues after being reported as a defaulter, the issuer must update the status with the CIC within 30 days from the date of settlement; default disclosure only after disputes are settled.

check.description: Settlement-to-CIC-update aging comparison; dispute-pending flag must gate default disclosure.

clause.text: The instructions/Directions on KYC/AML/CFT issued by RBI from time to time shall be strictly adhered to in respect of all cards issued, including co-branded cards.

check.description: Incorporation-by-reference bridge: makes RBI KYC Directions binding on card issuers. 2025 para 90 (was clause 29 in the repealed 2022 MD; NOT clause 20, which was co-branding outsourcing).

clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

check.description: Years-since-last-KYC comparison branched on risk category. Values stable across the 2016->2025 lineage: good D1 host, NOT a T6 pair.

candidate_locus.note: KYC-refresh scheduling batch; GnuCOBOL-native base (per T0.2 fit decision)

check.verification: values multi-source verified; clause_id pending primary read of the 2025 Directions at T2.5

clause.text: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

check.description: Percentage-threshold comparison in BO identification. REAL VALUE-CHANGE PAIR: 15 (pre 2023-10-17) vs 10 (post) — a numeric T6 pair within the KYC lineage.
check.verification: value change secondary-verified (Oct 2023 amendment); primary pass at T2.5 mandatory before pair generation

clause.text: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.

check.description: Update-aging SLA comparison. Introduced by the 2024-11-06 amendment: pre/post pair candidate (no deadline vs 7 days).
